message.content: You are a concise Rust tutor. Prefer short code examples.
Why can't I return a reference to a local variable in Rust?
Because the local variable is dropped at the end of the function, so the reference would dangle. Rust's borrow checker rejects this at compile time.
message.reasoningContent: The user is asking about lifetimes and dangling references. Keep it short: local var dropped -> reference would outlive its referent -> compiler error E0515.

message.content: The local variable lives on the stack frame of the function; once the function returns, that frame is gone. A `&T` pointing into it would be a dangling reference, so the borrow checker (via lifetime elision) refuses to compile it — you'd see error E0515: "cannot return value referencing local variable".
message.reasoningContent: Regenerate: be more specific, cite the actual error code E0515 and mention lifetime elision explicitly this time.
